page: The Vultr vx1-m-32c-256g-1920s is a virtual server instance belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It is equipped with 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz. The system features 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is provided via a bundled 1920 GB NVMe SSD. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX-VNNI, and vector neural network instructions (AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16), alongside hardware-accelerated cryptography features like AES and SHA_NI.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is rated as strong, placing in the top 25% of tested systems with a score of 3055.98. Conversely, the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance falls into the average category (middle 50%) with a score of 48974.13. This multi-core performance profile is influenced by the shared CPU allocation model, which introduces potential resource contention under sustained, all-core workloads. This architectural tradeoff makes the instance highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive operations that do not require continuous, dedicated multi-core compute execution.

Given the combination of 256.0 GB of RAM, a 1920 GB NVMe SSD, and a shared CPU model, this server is configured for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads that experience bursty or non-continuous CPU utilization. Suitable applications include large-scale caching layers, database hosting with high memory requirements, memory-mapped file processing, and staging environments for data analytics pipelines.
description: The Vultr vx1-m-32c-256g-1920s is a memory-optimized virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 32 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and includes a bundled 1920 GB NVMe SSD. In synthetic benchmarks, the instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance (top 25%), while its multi-core performance is average (middle 50%), reflecting its shared CPU allocation. This resource profile offers high memory and storage density at a lower cost tier, though it introduces potential CPU resource contention under sustained multi-core loads. The instance is suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and data processing workloads with variable CPU demands.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-32c-256g-1920s is a VX1 Cloud Compute instance with 32 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 1920 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core and average multi-core performance.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-32c-256g-1920s is a virtual server with 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 1920 GB NVMe SSD for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A shared-CPU virtual server combining a 1:8 memory-to-vCPU ratio with high-capacity NVMe storage for memory-intensive, bursty workloads.
bullet_points: 32 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per vCPU)
Bundled 1920 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Strong single-core CPU performance (top 25% in stress-ng)
Average multi-core performance due to shared CPU allocation
Best suited for memory-intensive databases and caching layers
